description: 'napuro' is the edible seed harvested from a cereal grass. Wheat, barley, oats, and rice all belong to the category. It can cover the standing crop or grain after harvest. 'napari' (rice) selects that particular staple; 'thinoe' (seed) concerns material kept or placed for growing.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n' and open into 'a'. Close the lips for unvoiced 'p', release into stressed high back rounded 'u', then tap or trill 'r' at the alveolar ridge and finish with middle-height back rounded 'o'.
sound_symbolism: The shared 'na.p' consonant frame links 'napuro' with 'napari' (rice). Different vowels keep the wider crop category and the particular staple distinct.

semantic_domains.nature: edible seed harvested from cereal grasses
semantic_domains.physical: grain handled as a crop, stored material, or food
